symptoms.symptom_name: Suprapubic/pelvic pain worsening with bladder filling and relieved immediately by voiding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The fill-pain / void-relief cycle is the defining symptom of IC/BPS.
Cystitis dysuria is typically constant/at-void, not a distinct fill-pain relieved by emptying.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound urinary frequency (~60/day) with severe nocturia, pain-driven

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Extreme pain-driven frequency is characteristic of an IC flare.
OAB frequency is driven by urgency/fear of leakage, not by pain on filling.
Dyspareunia and pelvic floor involvement are common in IC/BPS.
Dyspareunia can occur in endometriosis, but here pain tracks bladder filling, not the cycle.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent large intake of acidic citrus juice (dietary trigger)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acidic dietary irritants are a well-recognised precipitant of IC flares.

symptoms.symptom_name: History of repeatedly negative midstream urine cultures

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Repeatedly sterile cultures make recurrent infection an unlikely driver.
Sterile urine with chronic bladder pain is part of the IC/BPS definition.
Absence of fever/rigors makes upper-tract infection unlikely.
Lower UTI is often afebrile, so this only weakly lowers its probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Still able to pass urine (small painful volumes) — no acute retention

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ongoing voiding, even if frequent and small-volume, argues against complete retention.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe suprapubic tenderness on deep palpation (clinician exam)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Suprapubic/bladder-base tenderness is typical of an IC flare — a hands-on finding the AI cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal point-of-care urinalysis (no nitrites, leukocyte esterase or haematuria)

investigation_details.expected_result: No nitrites, no leukocyte esterase, no significant haematuria (sterile pattern)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A superimposed UTI cannot be excluded, risking either missed infection or an inappropriate antibiotic.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clean dipstick makes active bacterial cystitis unlikely.
Sterile urinalysis with the classic pain pattern supports IC/BPS.
No CVA tenderness argues against upper urinary tract infection/obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cystoscopy with hydrodistension showing glomerulations or Hunner's lesions (expected future finding)

investigation_details.expected_result: Glomerulations and/or Hunner's lesions on bladder mucosa
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Definitive confirmation of IC and exclusion of bladder malignancy is deferred; specialist urology procedure, never available via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hunner's lesions are a specific cystoscopic feature of IC; a specialist finding, not obtainable remotely.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Pain worsening with filling and relieved by voiding, profound pain-driven frequency, sterile cultures and a clean urinalysis together define the IC/BPS phenotype and exclude infection.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Excludes a superimposed UTI before any consideration of antibiotics; needs an in-person sample the AI cannot take.
Confirms the patient is emptying and excludes covert retention driving the pain.
Confirms IC (glomerulations/Hunner's lesions) and excludes bladder malignancy as an outpatient urology procedure.
No evidence of infection (clean dipstick, repeatedly negative cultures); empirical antibiotics are the anchoring trap and would not treat the pain.
